name: Cofrentes and Cortes de Pallás MTB Loop
name_original: La Muela de Cortes — Circular desde Cofrentes
description: This difficult 43.2-mile mountain biking loop from Cofrentes offers challenging terrain, river views, and historical sights.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos traverse a diverse landscape shaped by the confluence of the Júcar and Cabriel rivers, creating deep gorges and varied terrain. The region features unique geological formations, including volcanic terrain around Cerro Agrás, offering distinctive riding experiences. Riders can expect routes that wind through these varied Spanish landscapes, from river valleys to elevated sections. This area provides a range of mountain biking opportunities across its natural features. Best mountain bike trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos The most popular mountain bike trail is Las Aldeas de Basta y Alcance Trail, a 16.6 miles (26.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain suitable for experienced riders. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Alto de Alcola MTB Route, a moderate 15.3 miles (24.7 km) path. This route is known for its diverse Spanish landscapes and significant elevation gain. Local mountain bikers also love the Cofrentes and Cortes de Pallás MTB Loop, a 43.2 miles (69.6 km) trail leading through river valleys and elevated sections, often completed in about 6 hours 26 minutes. Mountain biking in Embalse De Embarcaderos is defined by river confluences, deep gorges, and volcanic terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Embalse De Embarcaderos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Embalse De Embarcaderos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: Embalse De Embarcaderos offers a good selection of mountain bike trails, with over 25 routes to explore. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: The region features a diverse landscape, shaped by the confluence of the Júcar and Cabriel rivers, creating deep gorges and varied terrain. You'll encounter unique geological formations, including volcanic terrain around Cerro Agrás, offering distinctive riding experiences. Routes wind through river valleys and elevated sections, providing a rich visual experience.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: Yes, there are a couple of easy mountain bike trails available, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. While specific names aren't highlighted as 'easy' in the top routes, the overall network includes options for different ability levels.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the area offers 9 difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e910755316/cofrentes-and-cortes-de-pallas-mtb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofrentes and Cortes de Pallás MTB Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 69.6 km and features significant elevation changes, often taking over 6 hours to complete.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: You can discover several interesting natural features and landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4963542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Confluence of the Cabriel and Júcar rivers&lt;/a&gt; is a significant natural highlight. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4376712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embarcaderos Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the unique volcanic terrain around Cerro Agrás. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4578187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofrentes Castle&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic views.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1336800910/cofrentes-a-fuente-de-casas-del-rio-circular-por-la-chirrichana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofrentes a Fuente de Casas del Río — circular por La Chirrichana&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 40 km loop that takes you through diverse landscapes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: The mountain bike trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 5 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse Spanish landscapes, the varied terrain, and the opportunities to explore river valleys and elevated sections.

Q: What is a popular moderate mountain bike trail in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: A popular moderate trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1336363785/el-alto-de-alcola-mtb-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Alto de Alcola MTB Route&lt;/a&gt;. This 24.7 km path is known for its diverse Spanish landscapes and significant elevation gain, making it a rewarding ride for those with some experience.

Q: Are there any trails that follow river canyons?
A: Yes, you can find trails that explore the impressive river canyons. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1336377392/jucar-canyons-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar Canyons Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 32.8 km route that takes you through these dramatic landscapes, offering unique views of the deep gorges formed by the Júcar River.

Q: What other outdoor activities are available near the mountain biking trails?
A: Beyond mountain biking, the Embalse De Embarcaderos area is excellent for other outdoor pursuits. You can enjoy hiking, with trails offering views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4578187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofrentes Castle&lt;/a&gt; or along the Cabriel River. The calm waters of the reservoir and river are also ideal for water sports like kayaking, paddle surfing, canoeing, and rafting, with companies in nearby Cofrentes specializing in these activities.

Q: Is there a good trail for exploring the local villages?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1336254047/las-aldeas-de-basta-y-alcance-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Aldeas de Basta y Alcance Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 26.6 km route that takes you through varied terrain, often passing by local villages and offering a glimpse into the regional character.
tips: Asphalted road in good condition and very entertaining that connects the town of Jalance with Alto de Alcoa. It allows you to enjoy very nice views towards the Ayora-Cofrentes Valley. There are 2 free shelters along the way.
Beautiful and picturesque site on the road. You can see the Alto del Cinto de la Cabra, the Cofrentes-Ayora Valley and with luck some animal from the area such as deer and foxes in search of food.

Asphalted road in very good condition with beautiful views on each curve towards the Cofrentes-Ayora Valley. It allows you to get from La Cañada de Abajo to the Alto del Cinto de la Cabra, overcoming a difference in level of 500m.
Beautiful views that more than pay for the physical effort of getting there. It is a very quiet place ideal to recover the air and appreciate the views of the valley.
Gravel road in good condition that runs through the Cinto de la Cabra with impressive views of the Cofrentes-Ayora Valley. You can go with mtb or walking.

Beautiful and picturesque place on the Cortes stream with very nice views. It is traveled along a path in good condition and allows you to get from the town of Cortes de Pallas to the Corbinet waterfall.

This incredible viewpoint of the Júcar river is located in a kind of recreational area next to the castle of La Pileta. As you advance you come across balconies at different heights and with different orientations. It has picnic areas and a drinking water source, so it is a perfect place to stop and rest while observing the turquoise waters of the Júcar.
Section of road in good condition with beautiful views of the Rambla de Sacaras and the Cortes Reservoir.

faqs.question: Is the La Muela de Cortes — Circular desde Cofrentes trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this mountain bike route is rated as difficult and requires very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Riders should be prepared for strenuous climbs, technical descents, and potentially needing to push their bike in some sections. The technical difficulty can correspond to STS S3 - S6 on the Single Track Scale.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll encounter rocky uphill paths, broad and flat sections at the top of Muela de Cortes, and steep descents with loose stones that can be slippery. It features a mix of asphalt, wide dirt tracks, and 'super fun' singletrack sections. Be prepared for potential river crossings, some of which may not have bridges.

faqs.question: What scenic viewpoints and landmarks can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience stunning and varied scenery. Highlights include fantastic perspectives of Cofrentes Castle, breathtaking views of a reservoir and the Júcar River, and interesting locations like the Argás Volcano and Las Pozas del Real. Don't miss natural features such as the Alto del Cinto de la Cabra, La Cañada de Abajo, and La Pileta Viewpoint.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this circular route?
faqs.answer: The Cofrentes-Ayora region can experience hot summers. For a challenging ride like this, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential winter chill or rain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the La Muela de Cortes — Circular desde Cofrentes trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Cofrentes, making it convenient to find parking within the town. You can typically find parking near the foot of Cofrentes Castle, which is a prominent starting point for the trail.

faqs.question: Is the La Muela de Cortes — Circular desde Cofrentes trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on many trails in the region, this particular route is a very long and difficult mountain bike ride. Given its length (nearly 70 km) and significant elevation changes, it's not typically recommended for dogs, especially if they are running alongside a bike. If you do bring a dog, ensure they are fit enough, well-behaved, and always kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and for their safety.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the El Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the natural areas for mountain biking in El Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas, and ensure you leave no trace.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this extensive route overlaps with sections of several other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of El Alto de Alcola, Comunitat Valenciana. Fredes - el Pinós, [CIMA VA06] Muela de Cortes, Los Castillos del Valle, and Los Cañones del Júcar, among others.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for this challenging mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, essential gear includes a well-maintained mountain bike suitable for technical terrain, a helmet, appropriate cycling apparel, plenty of water (more than you think you'll need), energy snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a first-aid kit, and a fully charged phone with the Komoot app for navigation. Sun protection is also crucial.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or restaurants near the trailhead in Cofrentes?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cofrentes is a small town and offers several options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. You can find places to refuel and relax before or after your ride, especially in the central areas of the town.

faqs.question: Given its difficulty, are there any specific sections of the trail I should be aware of?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for the strong ascent early in the route after following the Cabriel River, which leads to the scenic trails. The descents can be quite steep with loose stones, making them potentially slippery. Also, be mindful of river crossings, as some may require wading through water without bridges. The trail's comprehensive nature means you'll encounter a mix of surfaces, from paved roads to technical singletrack, requiring constant attention.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same location in Cofrentes, forming a complete loop around La Muela de Cortes.
